description: Filter-expression boolean cast: pass/drop is JSONata 2.0's boolean cast ($boolean) of the defined result, and an undefined result fails the node with TRANSFORM_UNDEFINED. Pins the cast table for empty composites and all-falsy arrays.

fixtures.name: Filter result [] casts to false (event dropped)
fixtures.description: A constant filter expression yielding an empty array drops the event: JSONata's boolean cast of [] is false.

fixtures.name: Filter result with all-falsy members casts to false (event dropped)
fixtures.description: An array is true only if some member casts to true; [0, ""] has none, so the event is dropped.

fixtures.name: Filter result with a truthy member casts to true (event passes)
fixtures.description: [0, 1] contains a member that casts to true, so the array casts to true and the event passes unchanged.

fixtures.name: Filter result {} casts to false (event dropped)
fixtures.description: The empty object casts to false under JSONata's boolean cast, so the event is dropped.

fixtures.name: Filter result empty string casts to false (event dropped)
fixtures.description: The empty string casts to false under JSONata's boolean cast, so the event is dropped.

fixtures.name: Undefined filter result fails the node with TRANSFORM_UNDEFINED
fixtures.description: An expression referencing a missing field yields undefined, which neither passes nor drops: the node fails per the Transforms rule and the error event routes per onError with the event attached.
